questions: What evidence do you have that all secular ideologies are incapable of producing good morals?
How does one define "good" morals, especially when considering different cultures or belief systems?
Can you provide examples of secular ideologies that have produced positive moral outcomes?
How does the author assume that just because someone calls another person a slur, they are aware of their history?
What evidence does the author provide to support the claim that all those who called others slurs were aware of their history?
Can you think of any other factors besides awareness that could influence why someone might be subjected to violence after being called a slur?
How do you know that people's obsession with state rights isn't actually an indication of their concern for democracy?
What evidence do you have to support your claim that a regional EC wouldn't face similar opposition?
Don't you think that people might still oppose a regional EC if they perceive it as limiting their state's autonomy?
How do you know that the current protests are not a reaction to something specific that happened during President Trump's term?
What evidence is there that past protests were solely related to former President Obama, rather than other factors such as broader societal issues?
Can you provide examples of how President Trump's policies have directly impacted marginalized communities in America?
How do you define "radical leftism"?
What evidence is there for your claim that conservatives ignore Jesus' teachings?
Is it accurate to label someone as a political ideology based on their teachings alone?
What evidence does the speaker provide to support their claim?
How does the speaker define "legally"?
Can you think of any instances where immigrants were allowed into the country without proper vetting?
What evidence do you have to support your claim?
How do you define "bombing"?
Are there any other factors you haven't considered when making this claim?
How do you know the government is incompetent?
What evidence do you have that a private entity could perform better than the government?
Can you provide an example where privatization led to improved outcomes compared to public management?
How do you know that the increase in the budget deficit and national debt didn't occur because of other factors not related to the stock market's performance?
What evidence do you have to support your claim that the transfer of wealth from public to private hands occurred during this time period?
Are there any alternative explanations for why the stock market might be performing well despite high levels of government borrowing?
How do you define "tranny"?
What evidence do you have that using the word "tranny" is offensive?
Can you provide examples of other words or phrases that are considered derogatory towards marginalized groups, such as LGBTQ+ individuals?
How do you define "mutual profit"?
Can you provide an example where a group was formed without some form of coercion or manipulation?
Do you think there's a limit to how large these groups can grow before they become unwieldy and less effective?
How do you know I assumed you were evil?
Can you explain why you think having a 'substantive dialogue' means not assuming someone is evil?
What evidence do you have that I failed to have a substantive dialogue?
How do you know you've considered every viewpoint?
What are some examples of "stupid selfish things" humans have done?
Are there any instances where humans have acted altruistically or for the greater good?
What evidence does the author provide to support their claim that "many, probably most" police officers go to the gym?
How does the author know that the average weight of male police officers is likely also 195?
Why might there be other factors besides going to the gym that could influence an officer's strength level?
How do you know these other factors aren't also influenced by NSA spying?
Can you provide evidence for how many lives have been saved due to NSA spying?
Are all the factors you mentioned equally relevant to the topic at hand?
How do you know your identity is accurate?
What evidence can you provide for identifying with four genders?
Can you explain why you don't feel like a man or woman?
How do you justify that repeating something multiple times makes it not a proof?

Can you provide an example where repeating something repeatedly is used as a form of proof?
What is your definition of "value"?
How does one determine if a currency has "attractiveness"?
Can you provide evidence for the claim that these altcoins were created solely for financial gain?
How does the author assume that the doctors were able to determine that they were "developing abnormally" while they were still in the womb?
What evidence does the author have to support their claim that they were recommended for abortion when they were still in the womb?
Is there any other possible explanation as to why the doctors might have recommended an abortion if indeed they had determined that the author was "developing abnormally"?
How does the author assume that Native Americans are not already part of the dominant culture?
What evidence can be provided to support the claim that Native Americans cannot change mainstream American culture?
Can other marginalized groups also be subjected to similar arguments, such as women or LGBTQ+ individuals?
How do you determine when someone's intentions are genuine?
What factors influence how we interpret someone's words?
Can you provide an example where context didn't play a role in determining intentions?
What evidence do you have to support your claim that British colonialism spread knowledge?
How does one determine when something has been "rooted" in a particular place or ideology, especially considering how complex history can be?
Can't other historical events also lay claim to being on the same path as liberalism?
What evidence does the author provide to support the claim that getting tattoos is synonymous with being impulsive?
How does the author define "living life on the edge"? Can they back up such a definition?
Is there any empirical data or research supporting the notion that people who have tattoos are more likely to be impulsive than those without them?
How does the author justify the idea that just because something is improbable doesn't mean it can't exist?
What evidence could someone provide to support the claim that the author has suspended their final judgment too easily?
Can you think of any other scenarios where someone might argue for agnosticism based solely on probability?
How do you know if you can handle a dog?
What makes you think your size will be enough to intimidate a dog?
Have you considered other factors when evaluating your chances against a dog?
How do you know your solution doesn't involve some form of groupthink?
What if people are resistant to change because they don't trust those who propose changes?
Can you provide evidence for the claim that opening up public discourse leads to more inclusive decision-making processes?
How do you define morality?
Can you provide an example of a time when you argued from a non-moral perspective?
What are some other ways you could have framed your argument without using "I know what you mean"?

What if the person with the pen was only pretending to try to stop the other person?

How do you know that 50% of police officers are bad?

How do you justify the idea that "it honestly seems fundamentally bad for anyone's to have privacy"?
What evidence can you provide to support your claim that the state is capable of using force legitimately?
Can you clarify how you define 'force' in this context, given that you are not addressing privacy from the state?
How do you know that the author's assumptions are "at best guesses"?
What evidence do you have that things could easily turn out differently without those assumptions?
Can you provide an example where one of these assumptions led to a logical fallacy in the argument?
How do you define "American"?
What would happen if someone else said they were 37% Scandinavian, 32% English, etc., but claimed to be a European American?
Why do you think being an American citizen means you can't also identify with other cultures or ethnicities?
How does the author define "white fragility"?
What evidence does the author provide to support their claim?
Is there any other factor besides race that could cause a person to feel uncomfortable or defensive when discussing race?
How do you justify your belief that treating capital gains the same as ordinary income would be beneficial?

How do you know the finish line in the space race isn't subjective?

How do you define "slur"?

What makes you say there's a difference?

How can you be sure your husband doesn't have other reasons?

Are all immigrations considered invasions or are some different?

What makes you so sure that all the participants are from a retirement community?
Can you explain why you assume the other participants weren't also over 50?

How does voting ensure representation?
Are there other ways to make a difference without voting?
What is your basis for saying that actual professionals are more accurate?
How do you know that the predictions of these professionals are not sensationalized?
